summary: An increase in arterial carbon dioxide stimulates the respiratory center more effectively by enhancing the activity of both peripheral and central chemoreceptors. Therefore, \(\boxed{\text{an increase}}\) in CO₂ acts as the primary stimulus for respiration. Agent1 concludes that increased arterial carbon dioxide is the most effective stimulant for the respiratory centers based on its ability to activate multiple types of chemoreceptors strongly when elevated. Thus, the correct answer aligns with this conclusion.
